# UC-13.6.12 · Grafana Explore Query Volume and Slow Query Detection

## Description

Ad-hoc Explore sessions can accidentally issue high-cardinality PromQL or full-table SQL scans that starve production datasources. Tracking latency and request bursts per user guides guardrails and training.

## Value

Protects shared metrics and warehouse clusters from exploratory query storms.

## Implementation

If Explore cannot be tagged, infer from `referer` containing `/explore`. Redact literal queries in logs; hash the query text field. Pair with datasource-side query logs for ground truth.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log" /api/ds/query
| search "Explore" OR "panelId=0" OR query_source="explore"
| eval ms=coalesce(query_duration_ms, duration_ms)
| bin _time span=10m
| stats count as qps_bucket, median(ms) as p50, perc95(ms) as p95 by _time, user, datasource_uid
| where p95 > 8000 OR qps_bucket > 200
| sort -p95
```

## Visualization

Scatter (qps vs p95), top users table, heatmap of datasource_uid.

## References

- [Grafana — Explore](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/explore/)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
